task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login.
The patient chart or profile for 'Horacio Santacruz' (Patient ID 20) is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the form to add a clinical SOAP note
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form or interface for adding a new Clinical Note or SOAP note for Horacio Santacruz is open.

task_completion.description: Enter the Subjective and Objective details
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subjective field contains 'Patient presents for follow-up after recent myocardial infarction. Reports mild fatigue and occasional shortness of breath with exertion.' and the Objective field contains 'BP 148/92, HR 78 bpm. Heart sounds regular. Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally.'

task_completion.description: Enter the Assessment and Plan details
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Assessment field contains 'Ischemic heart disease, post-MI follow-up.' and the Plan field contains 'Continue aspirin 81mg daily and statin therapy. Refer to cardiac rehabilitation. Follow up in 4 weeks.'
A confirmation message indicating the note was saved successfully, or the newly added SOAP note is visible in the patient's clinical history/records.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeMED web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the FreeMED UI, rather than a terminal window executing scripts or database commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual FreeMED application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches a genuine EMR/EHR web application (FreeMED) and not a local text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal shortcuts to insert data
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing SQL INSERT commands or curl requests to bypass the GUI.
